Eval 0 (dashboard): Real baseline got page-header + badges right but missed empty state and over-engineered with 10+ new CSS classes duplicating Pico's article semantics
Eval 1 (empty states): Both configs added else blocks, but skill produced contextual descriptions; baseline output generic 'No X found.' text
Eval 2 (audit): Largest delta — baseline reverted to inline JS filtering, breaking HTMX model; skill maintained server-side pattern throughout
The 'no-new-css-framework' assertion passes on both — not a useful discriminator for this skill
Most impactful skill guidance: .page-header pattern, badge usage, and HTMX ?partial= convention
Key skill insight from eval-0: without guidance, agents create custom CSS component systems; the skill correctly prevents this by pointing to Pico's semantic HTML
